feed.title: Pronounce — recent dictionary additions
feed.subtitle: How engineers actually pronounce project / product / jargon names. Community-maintained.

entry.title: T5 — T five
entry.summary: T5 is pronounced "T five" (/ˌtiː ˈfaɪv/). "T-five". Text-to-Text Transfer Transformer.

entry.title: PaLM — pom
entry.summary: PaLM is pronounced "pom" (/pɑːm/). Google brand: "pom" (palm tree); letter-by-letter also heard. Pathways Language Model.

entry.title: SAM — sam
entry.summary: SAM is pronounced "sam" (/sæm/). "sam" — name. Segment Anything Model.

entry.title: CLIP — clip
entry.summary: CLIP is pronounced "clip" (/klɪp/). "clip" the word. Contrastive Language-Image Pre-training.

entry.title: DINO — DEE noh
entry.summary: DINO is pronounced "DEE noh" (/ˈdiː noʊ/). "DEE-noh". Self-DIstillation with NO labels.

entry.title: ColBERT — col BURT
entry.summary: ColBERT is pronounced "col BURT" (/kɒlˈbɜːrt/). "col-BURT" — like Stephen Colbert. Contextualized Late interaction over BERT.

entry.title: HNSW — H N S W
entry.summary: HNSW is pronounced "H N S W" (/ˌeɪtʃ ɛn ɛs ˈdʌbəl juː/). Letters "H-N-S-W". Hierarchical Navigable Small World.

entry.title: Karpathy — kar PAH thee
entry.summary: Karpathy is pronounced "kar PAH thee" (/kɑːrˈpɑː θi/). "kar-PAH-thee". Andrej Karpathy.

entry.title: EVM — E V M
entry.summary: EVM is pronounced "E V M" (/ˌiː viː ˈɛm/). Letters "E-V-M". Ethereum Virtual Machine.

entry.title: ABI — A B I
entry.summary: ABI is pronounced "A B I" (/ˌeɪ biː ˈaɪ/). Letters "A-B-I". Application Binary Interface.

entry.title: dApp — D app
entry.summary: dApp is pronounced "D app" (/ˈdiː æp/). "D-app". Decentralized App.

entry.title: ENS — E N S
entry.summary: ENS is pronounced "E N S" (/ˌiː ɛn ˈɛs/). Letters "E-N-S". Ethereum Name Service.

entry.title: SLA — S L A
entry.summary: SLA is pronounced "S L A" (/ˌɛs ɛl ˈeɪ/). Letters "S-L-A". Service Level Agreement.

entry.title: SLO — S L O
entry.summary: SLO is pronounced "S L O" (/ˌɛs ɛl ˈoʊ/). Letters "S-L-O". Service Level Objective.

entry.title: SLI — S L I
entry.summary: SLI is pronounced "S L I" (/ˌɛs ɛl ˈaɪ/). Letters "S-L-I". Service Level Indicator.

entry.title: KPI — K P I
entry.summary: KPI is pronounced "K P I" (/ˌkeɪ piː ˈaɪ/). Letters "K-P-I".

entry.title: MTTR — M T T R
entry.summary: MTTR is pronounced "M T T R" (/ˌɛm tiː tiː ˈɑːr/). Letters "M-T-T-R". Mean Time To Recovery.

entry.title: MTBF — M T B F
entry.summary: MTBF is pronounced "M T B F" (/ˌɛm tiː biː ˈɛf/). Letters "M-T-B-F". Mean Time Between Failures.

entry.title: uvicorn — yoo vee corn
entry.summary: uvicorn is pronounced "yoo vee corn" (/ˌjuː viː ˈkɔːrn/). "yoo-vee-CORN". Python ASGI server.

entry.title: gunicorn — GUN i corn
entry.summary: gunicorn is pronounced "GUN i corn" (/ˈɡʌn i kɔːrn/). "GUN-uh-corn" — like a unicorn with a gun. Green Unicorn.

entry.title: WSGI — WIZ gee
entry.summary: WSGI is pronounced "WIZ gee" (/ˈwɪz ɡi/). "WIZ-gee" common; letter-by-letter also heard. Web Server Gateway Interface.

entry.title: WebKit — web kit
entry.summary: WebKit is pronounced "web kit" (/ˈwɛb kɪt/). "web-KIT". Apple browser engine.

entry.title: Big-O — big oh
entry.summary: Big-O is pronounced "big oh" (/ˌbɪɡ ˈoʊ/). "big-OH". Asymptotic upper bound notation.

entry.title: pub/sub — pub sub
entry.summary: pub/sub is pronounced "pub sub" (/ˈpʌb sʌb/). "pub-SUB". Publish/Subscribe pattern.

entry.title: OOM — O O M
entry.summary: OOM is pronounced "O O M" (/ˌoʊ oʊ ˈɛm/). Letters dominant; "oom" rare. Out Of Memory.
